result:
## Summary

Diagnosed and fixed a mid-pivot SEO mess on the Lumina (formerly InboxZap) SaaS site. The live URL was unreachable (known harness quirk), so I built and ran the app from source to gather real evidence, cross-referenced against the GSC export.

**Root cause:** the product was rebranded from InboxZap (email cleanup) to Lumina (AI meeting assistant), but only the homepage copy was updated — metadata, nav destinations, and the blog were left pointing at the old product or broken entirely.

**Fixed:**
- Site-wide `<title>`/description/OG tags said "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes" on every page despite Lumina content — rewrote to match the real product, added `metadataBase`/canonical
- `/features` and `/pricing` — linked from every page's nav and the homepage CTA — 404'd site-wide; built real pages for both
- 12 legacy blog posts with real GSC impressions (~13,900 combined) 404'd with no redirect — added 301s to `/blog` to preserve link equity
- Blog index was client-only, fetching a nonexistent `/api/posts` (crawlers saw only a loading spinner) — converted to a server component with real seed content, a `/blog/[slug]` route, and Article JSON-LD
- `/faq` permanently redirected to a `#faq` anchor that didn't exist — added the actual FAQ section
- `sitemap.ts` listed the broken pages and explicitly stubbed out blog posts — now dynamically includes everything real
- Added Organization/SoftwareApplication JSON-LD (none existed before)

Verified with a clean `npm run build` (zero errors, all routes statically generated) and re-curled every route to confirm status codes and rendered metadata. Full findings and a status-tracked action plan (including what's deliberately left for follow-up — content expansion, a real OG image asset, CWV measurement once deployed) are in `FULL-AUDIT-REPORT.md` and `ACTION-PLAN.md` in the workspace root.
